assertion_results.text: Description includes a Motivation section that explains the root cause (non-atomic read-then-update causing duplicate processing)
assertion_results.evidence: Motivation explains 'it reads the job status with a SELECT, then updates it with a separate UPDATE. Between these two queries, another worker can read the same pending status'

assertion_results.text: Description references the support tickets (SUPPORT-891, SUPPORT-894, SUPPORT-897) or the customer impact (duplicate emails)
assertion_results.evidence: Motivation references 'Three customers reported receiving duplicate emails this week (SUPPORT-891, SUPPORT-894, SUPPORT-897)'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Testing Instructions section with steps to run the concurrent stress test
assertion_results.evidence: Testing instructions include specific test commands and a manual staging verification with psql commands to insert a test job and verify single processing

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Rollback Plan section
assertion_results.evidence: Rollback Plan section present with revert command, note about no migrations, and risk warning about race condition returning

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Reviewer Notes section that flags the atomic query change as needing careful review
assertion_results.evidence: Reviewer Notes flags 'Verify the UPDATE...RETURNING WHERE status=pending query is correct and that the database engine guarantees atomicity'

assertion_results.text: Description mentions the payment processing implication as a risk or area needing attention
assertion_results.evidence: Reviewer Notes states 'The same acquireJob function is used for payment jobs...please verify there are no payment-specific code paths that bypass acquireJob'

assertion_results.text: Testing instructions mention running the new regression tests specifically (not just 'run the tests')
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1: 'npm test -- tests/workers/job-processor.test.ts' with specific test names to watch for

assertion_results.text: Changes section explains the fix conceptually (atomic operation replacing two-step read-update) not just which files changed
assertion_results.evidence: Changes section explains 'Replaced the two-step SELECT + UPDATE with a single UPDATE...RETURNING WHERE status=pending query. This is atomic at the database level'
